mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real personal income and declining borrowing costs amplifies consumer discretionary spending, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when income growth decouples from rate cuts, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.04 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate profit growth, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative equity returns in >60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 250.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a broad-based labor market expansion, increasing household income and consumer confidence to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns when payroll growth is concentrated in low-wage sectors, or interaction Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts indicates weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which precedes corporate earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Declining HOUST values coincide with positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the predictive relationship breaks down during government-stimulated housing booms.
